title: EAI_AGAIN error on HTTP requests to Railway
symptom: When making HTTP requests to a Railway-hosted FastAPI, n8n throws an EAI_AGAIN error. This indicates a DNS resolution issue for the specified hostname.
not_this_if: Request works in other tools like PowerShell
root_cause: The EAI_AGAIN error typically indicates a temporary DNS resolution failure. In this case, it was linked to an issue with Railway's TLS certificate, which prevented proper resolution of the service's hostname.
fix_steps: Ensure the HTTP request URL is correct and uses HTTPS.
Check for any security warnings related to the URL.
Retry the request after confirming the TLS certificate is valid.
agent_instruction: Verify the HTTP request URL and ensure it uses HTTPS without security warnings.
